basics.name: Danshu Jiang
basics.label: Biotechnology Undergraduate | Aspiring Bioinformatician
basics.summary: Undergraduate Biotechnology student at Hunan Agricultural University with hands-on research experience in plant tissue culture, virus detection, and virus-free technology design. Passionate about bridging biology and computation—applying Python and AI tools to solve biological problems. Seeking opportunities in bioinformatics to combine wet-lab expertise with computational analysis.

projects.name: Virus-Free Technology for Longya Lily (Lilium brownii)
projects.description: Designed and optimized a virus-free propagation protocol for Longya Lily, an economically important cultivar. Integrated meristem tip culture with thermotherapy and chemotherapy to eliminate viral pathogens.
highlights: Established shoot tip isolation and meristem culture protocols for Lilium brownii
Applied thermotherapy (37°C, 4 weeks) combined with ribavirin chemotherapy to enhance virus elimination efficiency
Achieved regeneration of virus-free plantlets with improved vigor compared to infected donor material
Designed the experimental workflow from explant sterilization through acclimatization

projects.name: Virus Detection in Lily — LMoV, LSV & CMV
projects.description: Screened lily samples for three major viral pathogens (Lily Mottle Virus, Lily Symptomless Virus, Cucumber Mosaic Virus) using molecular diagnostic techniques. Validated virus-free status of regenerated plants.
highlights: Performed RT-PCR detection of LMoV, LSV, and CMV from leaf tissue samples
Extracted total RNA and synthesized cDNA for multiplex screening
Compared virus incidence rates between field-collected and in-vitro-regenerated material
Documented detection protocols and maintained experimental records for reproducibility

projects.name: Plant Tissue Culture & Regeneration Systems
projects.description: Established and maintained in-vitro culture systems for lily and other horticultural crops. Investigated the effects of plant growth regulators on shoot proliferation and root induction.
highlights: Prepared MS media with varying concentrations of 6-BA, NAA, and IBA for organogenesis
Monitored and controlled aseptic conditions to minimize contamination rates below 5%
Optimized subculture intervals and rooting protocols across multiple lily genotypes
Acclimatized tissue-cultured plantlets in greenhouse conditions with >85% survival rate

projects.name: TNF Gene Bioinformatics Analysis
projects.description: Performed computational analysis of Tumor Necrosis Factor (TNF) gene across multiple species. Applied bioinformatics tools for sequence alignment, conserved domain identification, and phylogenetic analysis to understand evolutionary relationships and functional implications.
highlights: Retrieved TNF gene sequences from NCBI GenBank across multiple species
Performed multiple sequence alignment using Biopython and Clustal Omega
Identified conserved domains and predicted protein functional sites
Constructed phylogenetic tree to analyze TNF gene evolutionary relationships across species

projects.name: AI-Assisted Experimental Design for Lily Virus Elimination
projects.description: Leveraged large language models (ChatGPT, Claude) to optimize virus elimination protocols for lily. Applied AI tools for literature review, primer design, data analysis, and experimental documentation, significantly accelerating the research workflow.
highlights: Used ChatGPT and Claude Code to screen and summarize relevant virology literature, reducing literature review time by 60%
Applied LLM-assisted analysis of LMoV/LSV/CMV genome sequences to guide RT-PCR primer design
Maintained version-controlled experimental protocols and data records on GitHub
Employed AI tools for statistical analysis, figure generation, and scientific writing
